executive_summary: Canada is experiencing an unprecedented ransomware surge with over 100 Canadian organizations hit in the last 48 hours across all sectors. Notable victims include Canada Goose, National Money Mart (database leaked), Kids & Company childcare, Peterborough Public Health, Eastern Townships School Board, and the Municipality of North Perth. The Qilin ransomware group leads the attack wave with 20+ victims, followed by Play, Clop, and SafePay variants. High-impact sectors include public health, education, financial services, and critical infrastructure—indicating a coordinated mass exploitation event likely targeting a common vulnerability or service provider.

items.name: Qilin
items.targets: 20+ Canadian victims including Service Star Freightways, Kuzco Lighting, Waterloo Information Systems, Traffic Tech, Gradient Wind Engineering, Workers Health & Safety Centre, Bergmanis Preyra, Pre-Con Builders, Questica, Eastern Townships School Board, MG CPA, Watermark Beach Resort, CST Coal, BioPharma Services, NovAtel, Maheu&Maheu, Berts Electric, Capp Shupak across transportation, technology, public sector, education, healthcare, and energy sectors
items.ttps: T1486 (Data Encrypted for Impact), T1567 (Exfiltration Over Web Service), T1190 (Exploit Public-Facing Application)

items.name: Play
items.targets: 12+ Canadian victims including Select Tool, Equine Canada, Chemical Computing Group, Paisley Products, Makivik, ALLMAX, Security ONE Alarm Systems, Katch Kan, Turkstra Trusses in manufacturing, agriculture, technology sectors
items.ttps: T1486 (Data Encrypted for Impact), T1021 (Remote Services), T1078 (Valid Accounts)

items.name: Clop

items.ttps: T1486 (Data Encrypted for Impact), T1190 (Exploit Public-Facing Application), T1005 (Data from Local System)

canada_landscape.summary: Canada faces a critical mass-ransomware event with 100+ organizations compromised across all provinces and sectors on June 5, 2026. High-profile victims include Canada Goose (apparel), National Money Mart (financial services with confirmed database leak), Kids & Company (national childcare chain), Morison Insurance, Peterborough Public Health, Eastern Townships School Board, Municipality of North Perth, and Workers Health & Safety Centre. No CCCS advisories were identified in the 48-hour window despite this unprecedented attack volume.
canada_landscape.retailers: Canada Goose compromised by CoinbaseCartel ransomware; Active Green + Ross (automotive retail chain) hit by Sinobi ransomware
canada_landscape.financial: National Money Mart Company suffered database leak via Everest ransomware; Morison Insurance Brokers compromised by Sinobi; Trucash payment services hit by Lynx; multiple accounting firms (MG CPA, Pewarchuk CPA, Reidhurst Nagy) breached

report_2w.executive_summary: Google is actively patching an Android zero-day vulnerability (CVE-2025-48595) that is being exploited in the wild right now. Separately, password manager Dashlane confirmed hackers stole customer password vaults in a recent breach, putting user credentials at significant risk. A major supply chain attack compromised 31+ Red Hat npm packages with credential-stealing malware targeting cloud secrets and CI/CD pipelines. Palo Alto Networks GlobalProtect authentication bypass (CVE-2026-0257) is being exploited days after disclosure. Canada's CCCS issued an advisory about a security incident impacting GitHub internal repositories.

items.description: Android vulnerability under active exploitation, patched in June 2026 security update
items.cia_impact: Confidentiality: High, Integrity: High, Availability: Medium

items.description: Palo Alto Networks GlobalProtect authentication bypass flaw exploited days after disclosure
items.cia_impact: Confidentiality: High, Integrity: High, Availability: High

items.description: Unauthenticated RCE via SSTI in Simple SA Wirtualna Uczelnia redirectToUrl parameter, no patch available
items.cia_impact: Confidentiality: High, Integrity: High, Availability: High

items.name: Mini Shai-Hulud / Miasma / CanisterWorm (TeamPCP attributed)
items.targets: Red Hat npm packages, developer environments, cloud infrastructure
items.ttps: T1195.002 (Supply Chain Compromise: Software Supply Chain), T1552.001 (Credentials from Files), T1059.007 (Command and Scripting Interpreter: JavaScript), self-propagating worm behavior targeting npm packages

items.name: SideCopy (Pakistan-linked APT)
items.targets: Afghanistan Finance Ministry
items.ttps: T1566 (Phishing), T1204.002 (Malicious File), deployment of Xeno RAT

items.name: Medusa Ransomware
items.targets: SIGMA Technology (Canada operations mentioned)
items.ttps: T1486 (Data Encrypted for Impact), T1490 (Inhibit System Recovery)

items.ttps: T1486 (Data Encrypted for Impact), T1567.002 (Exfiltration to Cloud Storage)

items.ttps: T1204.001 (User Execution: Malicious Link), T1059.001 (PowerShell), ClickFix social engineering technique, deployment of NetSupport RAT

items.name: Stormous Ransomware

items.ttps: T1486 (Data Encrypted for Impact), T1567 (Exfiltration Over Web Service)

items.name: Pro-Iran Hackers

items.ttps: T1586.003 (Compromise Accounts: Cloud Accounts), social engineering via Meta AI bot exploitation, posting propaganda content

items.name: Unknown (JSMonoGlyphRAT campaign)
items.targets: US businesses via phishing
items.ttps: T1566.001 (Phishing: Spearphishing Attachment), T1059.007 (JavaScript), persistent backdoor deployment

items.name: Unknown (Nimbus RAT campaign)
items.targets: Organizations using Microsoft Teams and Google Drive
items.ttps: T1566 (Phishing), T1102 (Web Service), abuse of Microsoft Teams and Google Drive for C2, Java-based RAT deployment

canada_landscape.summary: The Canadian Centre for Cyber Security (CCCS) issued advisory AL26-013 regarding a security incident impacting GitHub internal repositories on May 29, 2026. Medusa ransomware targeted SIGMA Technology with Canadian operations mentioned. Beyond this, no direct breaches of major Canadian retailers or financial institutions were identified in this reporting period, though Canada faces broader geopolitical and digital sovereignty discussions.
